author: w3Lb
content: WHY: ripgrep earned 'golden' status partly by publishing rigorous comparative benchmarks with methodology. README claims 'Fast' with no numbers. SCOPE: xtask bench-compare subcommand that generates corpora (many small files; one huge file; deep tree with gitignored noise; CRLF corpus), runs ripsed vs GNU sed -i, sd, and perl -pi with hyperfine (warmups, N runs, JSON export), and emits a markdown table. Scenarios: literal replace across tree, regex with captures, delete lines, no-match tree (prescreen showcase, pairs with #98), single 500MB file. Publish: BENCHMARKS.md with methodology (versions, hardware, corpus generation script is in-repo so anyone can reproduce) + summary table linked from README. HONESTY RULE: publish losses too — if sd wins single-file cases pre-#97/#98, say so; re-run after those land. CI: not run in CI (noisy shared runners), xtask-only with documented invocation.
